summary: In a short interview excerpt, Lynda Resnick reflects on starting her advertising agency in the 1960s as a single mother. She discusses channeling her creativity into business, the importance of hiring people better than herself, and finding a partner who supports rather than feels threatened by her ambition.

principles.statement: Channel your creativity into business if you aren't a great fine artist.
principles.rationale: Business functions like copywriting and art direction offer daily outlets for creative expression and can lead to greater success than struggling in fine arts.
principles.source_quote: I'm very creative and so the creative spirit that I would've put into fine art…well, I'm not that great an artist. I'm doing much better in business than I would've as a fine artist.

principles.statement: Hire people who are better than you.
principles.rationale: Bringing in superior talent allows the business to grow and succeed at a high level.
principles.source_quote: And eventually, I could hire staff and I tried to get better people than me.

principles.statement: Choose a partner who understands your ambition and isn't threatened by it.
principles.rationale: A strong relationship requires mutual respect for each other's drive to succeed, without one person feeling outshined.
principles.source_quote: He just seemed like he was going to be successful and he would understand my drive to be successful and not be outshined by me.

signature_quotes.text: I'm doing much better in business than I would've as a fine artist.
signature_quotes.context: On applying her creative spirit to running an ad agency.

signature_quotes.text: And eventually, I could hire staff and I tried to get better people than me.
signature_quotes.context: On growing her ad agency in the 1960s.

signature_quotes.text: He just seemed like he was going to be successful and he would understand my drive to be successful and not be outshined by me.
signature_quotes.context: On why she chose to marry her husband, Stewart.

anti_patterns.description: Partnering with someone who is intimidated by your success.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It creates friction with your own ambition. You need a partner who understands your drive and won't feel outshined by your achievements.
